6	module
7	
8	public import Mathlib.Topology.Homeomorph.Lemmas
9	public import Mathlib.Topology.Sets.Closeds
10	
11	/-!
12	# Noetherian space
13	
14	A Noetherian space is a topological space that satisfies any of the following equivalent conditions:
15	- `WellFounded ((· > ·) : TopologicalSpace.Opens α → TopologicalSpace.Opens α → Prop)`
16	- `WellFounded ((· < ·) : TopologicalSpace.Closeds α → TopologicalSpace.Closeds α → Prop)`
17	- `∀ s : Set α, IsCompact s`
18	- `∀ s : TopologicalSpace.Opens α, IsCompact s`
19	
20	The first is chosen as the definition, and the equivalence is shown in
21	`TopologicalSpace.noetherianSpace_TFAE`.
22	
23	Many examples of Noetherian spaces come from algebraic topology. For example, the underlying space
24	of a Noetherian scheme (e.g., the spectrum of a Noetherian ring) is Noetherian.
25	
26	## Main Results
27	
28	- `TopologicalSpace.NoetherianSpace.set`: Every subspace of a Noetherian space is Noetherian.
29	- `TopologicalSpace.NoetherianSpace.isCompact`: Every set in a Noetherian space is a compact set.
30	- `TopologicalSpace.noetherianSpace_TFAE`: Describes the equivalent definitions of Noetherian
31	  spaces.
32	- `TopologicalSpace.NoetherianSpace.range`: The image of a Noetherian space under a continuous map
33	  is Noetherian.
34	- `TopologicalSpace.NoetherianSpace.iUnion`: The finite union of Noetherian spaces is Noetherian.
35	- `TopologicalSpace.NoetherianSpace.discrete`: A Noetherian and Hausdorff space is discrete.
36	- `TopologicalSpace.NoetherianSpace.exists_finset_irreducible`: Every closed subset of a Noetherian
37	  space is a finite union of irreducible closed subsets.
38	- `TopologicalSpace.NoetherianSpace.finite_irreducibleComponents`: The number of irreducible
39	  components of a Noetherian space is finite.
40	
41	-/
42	
43	@[expose] public section
44	
45	open Topology
46	
47	variable (α β : Type*) [TopologicalSpace α] [TopologicalSpace β]
48	
49	namespace TopologicalSpace
50	
51	/-- Type class for Noetherian spaces. It is defined to be spaces whose open sets satisfies ACC. -/
52	abbrev NoetherianSpace : Prop := WellFoundedGT (Opens α)
53	
54	theorem noetherianSpace_iff_opens : NoetherianSpace α ↔ ∀ s : Opens α, IsCompact (s : Set α) := by
55	  rw [NoetherianSpace, CompleteLattice.wellFoundedGT_iff_isSupFiniteCompact,
56	    CompleteLattice.isSupFiniteCompact_iff_all_elements_compact]
57	  exact forall_congr' Opens.isCompactElement_iff
58	
59	instance (priority := 100) NoetherianSpace.compactSpace [h : NoetherianSpace α] : CompactSpace α :=
60	  ⟨(noetherianSpace_iff_opens α).mp h ⊤⟩
61	
62	variable {α β}
63	
64	/-- In a Noetherian space, all sets are compact. -/
65	protected theorem NoetherianSpace.isCompact [NoetherianSpace α] (s : Set α) : IsCompact s := by
66	  refine isCompact_iff_finite_subcover.2 fun U hUo hs => ?_
67	  rcases ((noetherianSpace_iff_opens α).mp ‹_› ⟨⋃ i, U i, isOpen_iUnion hUo⟩).elim_finite_subcover U
68	    hUo Set.Subset.rfl with ⟨t, ht⟩
69	  exact ⟨t, hs.trans ht⟩
70	
71	protected theorem _root_.Topology.IsInducing.noetherianSpace [NoetherianSpace α] {i : β → α}
72	    (hi : IsInducing i) : NoetherianSpace β :=
73	  (noetherianSpace_iff_opens _).2 fun _ => hi.isCompact_iff.2 (NoetherianSpace.isCompact _)
74	
75	@[stacks 0052 "(1)"]
76	instance NoetherianSpace.set [NoetherianSpace α] (s : Set α) : NoetherianSpace s :=
77	  IsInducing.subtypeVal.noetherianSpace
78	
79	variable (α) in
80	open List in
81	theorem noetherianSpace_TFAE :
82	    TFAE [NoetherianSpace α,
83	      WellFoundedLT (Closeds α),
84	      ∀ s : Set α, IsCompact s,
85	      ∀ s : Opens α, IsCompact (s : Set α)] := by
86	  tfae_have 1 ↔ 2 := by
87	    simp_rw [isWellFounded_iff]
88	    exact Opens.compl_bijective.2.wellFounded_iff (@OrderIso.compl (Set α)).lt_iff_lt.symm
89	  tfae_have 1 ↔ 4 := noetherianSpace_iff_opens α
90	  tfae_have 1 → 3 := @NoetherianSpace.isCompact α _
91	  tfae_have 3 → 4 := fun h s => h s
92	  tfae_finish
93	
94	theorem noetherianSpace_iff_isCompact : NoetherianSpace α ↔ ∀ s : Set α, IsCompact s :=
95	  (noetherianSpace_TFAE α).out 0 2
96	
97	instance [NoetherianSpace α] : WellFoundedLT (Closeds α) :=
98	  Iff.mp ((noetherianSpace_TFAE α).out 0 1) ‹_›
99	
100	instance {α} : NoetherianSpace (CofiniteTopology α) := by
101	  simp only [noetherianSpace_iff_isCompact, isCompact_iff_ultrafilter_le_nhds,
102	    CofiniteTopology.nhds_eq, Ultrafilter.le_sup_iff, Filter.le_principal_iff]
103	  intro s f hs
104	  rcases f.le_cofinite_or_eq_pure with (hf | ⟨a, rfl⟩)
105	  · rcases Filter.nonempty_of_mem hs with ⟨a, ha⟩
106	    exact ⟨a, ha, Or.inr hf⟩
107	  · exact ⟨a, hs, Or.inl le_rfl⟩
108	
109	theorem noetherianSpace_of_surjective [NoetherianSpace α] (f : α → β) (hf : Continuous f)
110	    (hf' : Function.Surjective f) : NoetherianSpace β :=
111	  noetherianSpace_iff_isCompact.2 <| (Set.image_surjective.mpr hf').forall.2 fun s =>
112	    (NoetherianSpace.isCompact s).image hf
113	
114	theorem noetherianSpace_iff_of_homeomorph (f : α ≃ₜ β) : NoetherianSpace α ↔ NoetherianSpace β :=
115	  ⟨fun _ => noetherianSpace_of_surjective f f.continuous f.surjective,
116	    fun _ => noetherianSpace_of_surjective f.symm f.symm.continuous f.symm.surjective⟩
117	
118	theorem NoetherianSpace.range [NoetherianSpace α] (f : α → β) (hf : Continuous f) :
119	    NoetherianSpace (Set.range f) :=
120	  noetherianSpace_of_surjective (Set.rangeFactorization f) (hf.subtype_mk _)
121	    Set.rangeFactorization_surjective

123	theorem noetherianSpace_set_iff (s : Set α) :
124	    NoetherianSpace s ↔ ∀ t, t ⊆ s → IsCompact t := by
125	  simp only [noetherianSpace_iff_isCompact, IsEmbedding.subtypeVal.isCompact_iff,
126	    Subtype.forall_set_subtype]
127	
128	@[simp]
129	theorem noetherian_univ_iff : NoetherianSpace (Set.univ : Set α) ↔ NoetherianSpace α :=
130	  noetherianSpace_iff_of_homeomorph (Homeomorph.Set.univ α)
131	
132	theorem NoetherianSpace.iUnion {ι : Type*} (f : ι → Set α) [Finite ι]
133	    [hf : ∀ i, NoetherianSpace (f i)] : NoetherianSpace (⋃ i, f i) := by
134	  simp_rw [noetherianSpace_set_iff] at hf ⊢
135	  intro t ht
136	  rw [← Set.inter_eq_left.mpr ht, Set.inter_iUnion]
137	  exact isCompact_iUnion fun i => hf i _ Set.inter_subset_right
138	
139	-- This is not an instance since it makes a loop with `t2_space_discrete`.
140	theorem NoetherianSpace.discrete [NoetherianSpace α] [T2Space α] : DiscreteTopology α :=
141	  ⟨eq_bot_iff.mpr fun _ _ => isClosed_compl_iff.mp (NoetherianSpace.isCompact _).isClosed⟩
142	
143	attribute [local instance] NoetherianSpace.discrete
144	
145	/-- Spaces that are both Noetherian and Hausdorff are finite. -/
146	theorem NoetherianSpace.finite [NoetherianSpace α] [T2Space α] : Finite α :=
147	  Finite.of_finite_univ (NoetherianSpace.isCompact Set.univ).finite_of_discrete
148	
149	instance (priority := 100) Finite.to_noetherianSpace [Finite α] : NoetherianSpace α :=
150	  ⟨Finite.wellFounded_of_trans_of_irrefl _⟩

152	/-- In a Noetherian space, every closed set is a finite union of irreducible closed sets. -/
153	theorem NoetherianSpace.exists_finite_set_closeds_irreducible [NoetherianSpace α] (s : Closeds α) :
154	    ∃ S : Set (Closeds α), S.Finite ∧ (∀ t ∈ S, IsIrreducible (t : Set α)) ∧ s = sSup S := by
155	  apply wellFounded_lt.induction s; clear s
156	  intro s H
157	  rcases eq_or_ne s ⊥ with rfl | h₀
158	  · use ∅; simp
159	  · by_cases h₁ : IsPreirreducible (s : Set α)
160	    · replace h₁ : IsIrreducible (s : Set α) := ⟨Closeds.coe_nonempty.2 h₀, h₁⟩
161	      use {s}; simp [h₁]
162	    · simp only [isPreirreducible_iff_isClosed_union_isClosed, not_forall, not_or] at h₁
163	      obtain ⟨z₁, z₂, hz₁, hz₂, h, hz₁', hz₂'⟩ := h₁
164	      lift z₁ to Closeds α using hz₁
165	      lift z₂ to Closeds α using hz₂
166	      rcases H (s ⊓ z₁) (inf_lt_left.2 hz₁') with ⟨S₁, hSf₁, hS₁, h₁⟩
167	      rcases H (s ⊓ z₂) (inf_lt_left.2 hz₂') with ⟨S₂, hSf₂, hS₂, h₂⟩
168	      refine ⟨S₁ ∪ S₂, hSf₁.union hSf₂, Set.union_subset_iff.2 ⟨hS₁, hS₂⟩, ?_⟩
169	      rwa [sSup_union, ← h₁, ← h₂, ← inf_sup_left, left_eq_inf]
170	
171	/-- In a Noetherian space, every closed set is a finite union of irreducible closed sets. -/
172	theorem NoetherianSpace.exists_finite_set_isClosed_irreducible [NoetherianSpace α]
173	    {s : Set α} (hs : IsClosed s) : ∃ S : Set (Set α), S.Finite ∧
174	      (∀ t ∈ S, IsClosed t) ∧ (∀ t ∈ S, IsIrreducible t) ∧ s = ⋃₀ S := by
175	  lift s to Closeds α using hs
176	  rcases NoetherianSpace.exists_finite_set_closeds_irreducible s with ⟨S, hSf, hS, rfl⟩
177	  refine ⟨(↑) '' S, hSf.image _, Set.forall_mem_image.2 fun S _ ↦ S.2, Set.forall_mem_image.2 hS,
178	    ?_⟩
179	  lift S to Finset (Closeds α) using hSf
180	  simp [← Finset.sup_id_eq_sSup, Closeds.coe_finset_sup]

182	/-- In a Noetherian space, every closed set is a finite union of irreducible closed sets. -/
183	theorem NoetherianSpace.exists_finset_irreducible [NoetherianSpace α] (s : Closeds α) :
184	    ∃ S : Finset (Closeds α), (∀ k : S, IsIrreducible (k : Set α)) ∧ s = S.sup id := by
185	  simpa [Set.exists_finite_iff_finset, Finset.sup_id_eq_sSup]
186	    using NoetherianSpace.exists_finite_set_closeds_irreducible s

namespace GroupScheme

/-- Helper (iter-188): in a **Noetherian** topological space `α`, the set of
connected components is finite.

Proof: the map `irreducibleComponents α → ConnectedComponents α` sending each
irreducible component `C` to the connected component of an arbitrary chosen
point of `C` is well-defined (because `C` is preconnected, hence contained in
a single connected component) and surjective (because every point lies in
some irreducible component, namely `irreducibleComponent x`, which is a
subset of `connectedComponent x`). The source is finite by
`TopologicalSpace.NoetherianSpace.finite_irreducibleComponents`, so the
target is finite by `Finite.of_surjective`. -/
private lemma noetherianSpace_finite_connectedComponents
    {α : Type*} [TopologicalSpace α] [TopologicalSpace.NoetherianSpace α] :
    Finite (ConnectedComponents α) := by
  classical
  have hfin : (irreducibleComponents α).Finite :=
    TopologicalSpace.NoetherianSpace.finite_irreducibleComponents
  haveI : Finite ↥(irreducibleComponents α) := hfin.to_subtype
  refine Finite.of_surjective
    (fun C : ↥(irreducibleComponents α) =>
      (ConnectedComponents.mk : α → ConnectedComponents α)
        ((C.property : Maximal IsIrreducible C.val).prop.nonempty.some)) ?_
  intro c
  obtain ⟨x, rfl⟩ := ConnectedComponents.surjective_coe c
  refine ⟨⟨irreducibleComponent x, irreducibleComponent_mem_irreducibleComponents x⟩, ?_⟩
  rw [ConnectedComponents.coe_eq_coe]
  refine (connectedComponent_eq ?_).symm
  exact irreducibleComponent_subset_connectedComponent
    (isIrreducible_irreducibleComponent (x := x)).nonempty.some_mem

/-- Helper (iter-188): in a **Noetherian** topological space `α`, each
connected component is open.

Proof: `ConnectedComponents α` is totally disconnected
(`ConnectedComponents.totallyDisconnectedSpace`), hence T1
(`TotallyDisconnectedSpace.t1Space`), and finite by
`noetherianSpace_finite_connectedComponents`. A finite T1 space is discrete
(`Finite.instDiscreteTopology`), so the singleton
`{ConnectedComponents.mk x}` is open in the quotient. The preimage of this
singleton under the continuous quotient map `ConnectedComponents.mk` is
`connectedComponent x` (`connectedComponents_preimage_singleton`), which is
therefore open. -/
private lemma noetherianSpace_isOpen_connectedComponent
    {α : Type*} [TopologicalSpace α] [TopologicalSpace.NoetherianSpace α]
    (x : α) :
    IsOpen (connectedComponent x) := by
  haveI : Finite (ConnectedComponents α) := noetherianSpace_finite_connectedComponents
  haveI : DiscreteTopology (ConnectedComponents α) := inferInstance
  have h := (isOpen_discrete
    ({(ConnectedComponents.mk x : ConnectedComponents α)} : Set _)).preimage
    ConnectedComponents.continuous_coe
  rwa [connectedComponents_preimage_singleton] at h

/-- Helper (iter-188): the **`LocallyConnectedSpace` instance** for the
underlying topological space of a `k`-scheme `G.left` whose structural
morphism `G.hom : G.left ⟶ Spec k` is locally of finite type.

The substantive content is EGA I 6.1.9: a locally Noetherian topological
space has open connected components — equivalently, is locally connected.
Pushed through the chain: `Spec k` is Noetherian (a field has Noetherian
spectrum); `LocallyOfFiniteType G.hom + IsLocallyNoetherian (Spec k) ⟹
IsLocallyNoetherian G.left` (Mathlib's
`AlgebraicGeometry.LocallyOfFiniteType.isLocallyNoetherian`); and the
implication `IsLocallyNoetherian X ⟹ LocallyConnectedSpace X.toTopCat`
is the iter-188 project-side helper (`noetherianSpace_finite_connectedComponents`
+ `noetherianSpace_isOpen_connectedComponent`).

The classical proof: each point `y ∈ G.left` has an open affine
neighbourhood `W = Spec R` with `R` Noetherian, hence `|W|` is a Noetherian
topological space; in a Noetherian space, each `connectedComponent y` is
clopen (finite irreducible components ⟹ finite connected components ⟹
finite T1 quotient ⟹ discrete quotient ⟹ singletons clopen in quotient ⟹
preimages clopen). Pulled back along the open inclusion `W ↪ G.left`,
this gives an open connected neighbourhood of `y` inside any open `F`
containing `y`. -/
private instance identityComponent_locallyConnectedSpace
    {k : Type u} [Field k]
    (G : Over (Spec (.of k)))
    [LocallyOfFiniteType G.hom] :
    LocallyConnectedSpace G.left := by
  haveI : IsLocallyNoetherian G.left := LocallyOfFiniteType.isLocallyNoetherian G.hom
  rw [locallyConnectedSpace_iff_connectedComponentIn_open]
  intro F hF x hxF
  rw [isOpen_iff_mem_nhds]
  intro y hy
  -- y ∈ connectedComponentIn F x ⊆ F.
  have hyF : y ∈ F := connectedComponentIn_subset F x hy
  -- Find an affine open W with y ∈ W ⊆ F.
  obtain ⟨W, hW, hyW, hWF⟩ :=
    exists_isAffineOpen_mem_and_subset (X := G.left) (U := ⟨F, hF⟩) hyF
  haveI : IsNoetherianRing Γ(G.left, W) :=
    IsLocallyNoetherian.component_noetherian ⟨W, hW⟩
  haveI hNoethW : TopologicalSpace.NoetherianSpace W :=
    noetherianSpace_of_isAffineOpen W hW
  -- The connected component of `y` in `W` (as a subspace) lifts to an open
  -- subset of `G.left` contained in `W ⊆ F`, containing `y`.
  have hcc_open : IsOpen (connectedComponentIn (W : Set G.left) y) := by
    rw [connectedComponentIn_eq_image hyW]
    exact W.isOpen.isOpenMap_subtype_val _
      (noetherianSpace_isOpen_connectedComponent _)
  -- Replace `connectedComponentIn F x` with `connectedComponentIn F y` via
  -- `connectedComponentIn_eq`, then exhibit `connectedComponentIn W y` as
  -- an open neighbourhood of `y` contained in `connectedComponentIn F y`.
  rw [connectedComponentIn_eq hy]
  refine Filter.mem_of_superset
    (hcc_open.mem_nhds (mem_connectedComponentIn hyW)) ?_
  refine isPreconnected_connectedComponentIn.subset_connectedComponentIn
    (mem_connectedComponentIn hyW) ?_
  exact subset_trans (connectedComponentIn_subset _ _) hWF

private instance identityComponent_locallyConnectedSpace
    {k : Type u} [Field k]
    (G : Over (Spec (.of k)))
    [LocallyOfFiniteType G.hom] :
    LocallyConnectedSpace G.left := by
  haveI : IsLocallyNoetherian G.left := LocallyOfFiniteType.isLocallyNoetherian G.hom
  rw [locallyConnectedSpace_iff_subsets_isOpen_isConnected]
  intro x F hF
  -- Extract an open neighbourhood `T ⊆ F` of `x`.
  obtain ⟨T, hTF, hT, hxT⟩ := mem_nhds_iff.mp hF
  -- Find an affine open `W ⊆ T` with `x ∈ W`.
  obtain ⟨W, hW, hxW, hWT⟩ :=
    exists_isAffineOpen_mem_and_subset (X := G.left) (U := ⟨T, hT⟩) hxT
  haveI : IsNoetherianRing Γ(G.left, W) :=
    IsLocallyNoetherian.component_noetherian ⟨W, hW⟩
  haveI hNoethW : TopologicalSpace.NoetherianSpace W :=
    noetherianSpace_of_isAffineOpen W hW
  -- Inside `W` (Noetherian) the connected component of `x` is open; its image
  -- under the open inclusion `W ↪ G.left` is the connected open neighbourhood
  -- we want.
  let xW : W := ⟨x, hxW⟩
  refine ⟨(Subtype.val : W → G.left) '' connectedComponent xW, ?_, ?_, ?_, ?_⟩
  · rintro _ ⟨z, _, rfl⟩
    exact hTF (hWT z.2)
  · exact W.isOpen.isOpenMap_subtype_val _
      (noetherianSpace_isOpen_connectedComponent xW)
  · exact ⟨xW, mem_connectedComponent, rfl⟩
  · exact isConnected_connectedComponent.image _ continuous_subtype_val.continuousOn

  two Mathlib-generic helpers + the scheme-specific wrapper.

     Noetherian space has finitely many connected components, via
     surjection `irreducibleComponents α → ConnectedComponents α`
     (well-defined because each irreducible set is preconnected,
     surjective because `irreducibleComponent x ⊆ connectedComponent x`).
     Source is finite by `TopologicalSpace.NoetherianSpace.finite_irreducibleComponents`.
  2. `noetherianSpace_isOpen_connectedComponent` (~9 LOC):
     `ConnectedComponents α` is `TotallyDisconnectedSpace`

     quotient; preimage clopen via `connectedComponents_preimage_singleton`.
  3. Scheme-specific wrapper (~25 LOC): use

     (`exists_isAffineOpen_mem_and_subset`). `LocallyOfFiniteType G.hom`
     + `IsLocallyNoetherian (Spec k)` gives `IsLocallyNoetherian G.left`
     (`LocallyOfFiniteType.isLocallyNoetherian`); affine open + Noetherian
     ring ⟹ Noetherian topological space (`noetherianSpace_of_isAffineOpen`).
     In `↥W` (Noetherian), `connectedComponent (⟨x, hxW⟩ : ↥W)` is open
     (Helper 2). Its image under `Subtype.val : ↥W → G.left` (open map
     since `W` is open: `IsOpen.isOpenMap_subtype_val`) is the wanted
     open connected neighbourhood of `x` inside `F`.
